records.ety: [LL. deplumare; L. de- + plumare to cover with feathers, pluma feather: cf. deplumis featherless, and F. dplumer.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deplumed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Depluming.]

defs: To strip or pluck off the feather of; to deprive of of plumage.
quotes: On the depluming of the pope every bird had his own feather.
The exposure and depluming of the leading humbugs of the age.
